questions.question: Prove that the opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal.
questions.answer: In parallelogram ABCD, AB || CD and AD || BC. Using alternate interior angles, ∠A = ∠C and ∠B = ∠D.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.
questions.answer: Let ABCD be a parallelogram, diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. Using triangle congruence, show AO = CO and BO = DO.

questions.question: Prove that opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence in parallelogram ABCD to show AB = CD and AD = BC.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle are equal.
questions.answer: In rectangle ABCD, use Pythagoras theorem in △ABC: AC² = AB² + BC². Similarly, BD² = AB² + BC², so AC = BD.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles.
questions.answer: Let ABCD be rhombus, diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. Triangles AOB and COD are congruent, ∴ ∠AOB = 90°.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a square bisect each other at right angles and are equal.
questions.answer: Since square is a rectangle and rhombus, combine properties: diagonals equal and perpendicular.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of squares of diagonals of a parallelogram equals the sum of squares of its sides multiplied by 2.
questions.answer: Let sides be a and b, diagonals p and q. Using vector method or parallelogram law, p² + q² = 2(a² + b²).

questions.question: Prove that in a parallelogram, each diagonal divides it into two congruent triangles.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by a diagonal share a common base and height; use congruence criteria to prove equality.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if its diagonals bisect each other.
questions.answer: Let diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. If AO = CO and BO = DO, show opposite sides are equal using triangle congruence, hence parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that a rectangle is a parallelogram with equal diagonals.
questions.answer: Since rectangle is parallelogram with all angles 90°, use Pythagoras theorem to show diagonals equal.

questions.question: Prove that a rhombus is a parallelogram with diagonals perpendicular.
questions.answer: By definition, rhombus has all sides equal; prove opposite sides parallel, hence parallelogram; diagonals perpendicular by symmetry.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a kite are perpendicular.
questions.answer: Draw kite ABCD with equal adjacent sides; use congruent triangles formed by diagonals to show intersection at 90°.

questions.question: Prove that a square is a rectangle with equal sides.
questions.answer: By definition, square has all sides equal and angles 90°, which satisfies rectangle properties.

questions.question: Prove that a square is a rhombus with right angles.
questions.answer: Since square has all sides equal and angles 90°, it satisfies rhombus properties with right angles.

questions.question: Prove that the midpoints of the sides of any quadrilateral form a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Join midpoints; use vector method or midpoint theorem to prove opposite sides of new quadrilateral parallel.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram is a rectangle if its diagonals are equal.
questions.answer: Let ABCD be parallelogram, AC = BD. Show using congruent triangles that all angles = 90°, hence rectangle.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram is a rhombus if its diagonals are perpendicular.
questions.answer: Let ABCD be parallelogram with perpendicular diagonals. Using congruent triangles, show all sides equal, hence rhombus.

questions.question: Prove that the opposite sides of a trapezium are not necessarily equal.
questions.answer: Draw trapezium ABCD with AB || CD and show AD ≠ BC in general, so opposite sides may not be equal.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of angles on the same side of a transversal in a parallelogram is 180°.
questions.answer: Use AB || CD, AD || BC, alternate interior and co-interior angles to show ∠A + ∠D = 180°.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhombus bisect opposite angles.
questions.answer: Draw rhombus ABCD; diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. Use triangle congruence to show ∠A = ∠C bisected, ∠B = ∠D bisected.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle bisect each other.
questions.answer: In rectangle ABCD, draw diagonals AC and BD intersecting at O. Triangles formed are congruent; hence AO = CO and BO = DO.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if its opposite sides are equal.
questions.answer: Let ABCD have AB = CD and AD = BC. Using triangle congruence, prove opposite sides parallel, hence parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if its opposite angles are equal.
questions.answer: Let ∠A = ∠C and ∠B = ∠D. Draw diagonals, use triangle congruence to show opposite sides parallel, hence parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if one pair of opposite sides is parallel and equal.
questions.answer: Use properties of triangles formed by diagonal to show other sides parallel; hence parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral is 360° using triangles.
questions.answer: Divide quadrilateral into two triangles using a diagonal. Sum of interior angles of two triangles = 180° + 180° = 360°.
